name: environmental_licensing_backlog_stonehaven_tru_loy
premise: A Environmental Policy response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving environmental licensing backlog stonehaven tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Mei Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Mei Ishikawa, accountable for a defensible call in Environmental Policy when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Leila Santos
agents.identity: I am Leila Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environmental Policy.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Mei Ishikawa must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Mei Ishikawa do?
